steps.title: Find the radius slope
steps.content:
The radius runs from the center $(-2,3)$ to the tangent point $(4,-5)$. Its slope is
$$\frac{-5-3}{4-(-2)}=\frac{-8}{6}=-\frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Write the tangent line
steps.content:
A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius, so its slope is 
$$
\frac{3}{4}
$$ Use the tangent point $(4,-5)$.
$$y+5=\frac{3}{4}(x-4)$$
$$y=\frac{3}{4}x-8$$

steps.title: Test the listed point
steps.content: Substitute $x=12$ into the tangent line equation. $$y=\frac{3}{4}(12)-8=9-8=1$$ So $(12,1)$ lies on line $t$. The answer is C.
